findings.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/static/js/upload.js:157,216 - insertAdjacentHTML() and innerHTML used with unsanitized user-controlled data (photo.originalFileName). Photo filenames are inserted into DOM without HTML escaping.

findings.name: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
findings.description: The product parses a formatted message or structure, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a length field that is inconsistent with the actual length of the associated data.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - file.getContentType() called without null check before .toLowerCase(), potential NullPointerException if content type is null

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.
findings.evidence: application.properties:12 - spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; application-docker.properties:4 - spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; docker-compose.yml:7 - ORACLE_PASSWORD=photoalbum

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - File type validation relies on MIME type checking from client-supplied Content-Type header without magic number/signature verification.

findings.name: Missing Initialization of a Variable
findings.description: The product does not initialize critical variables, which causes the execution environment to use unexpected values.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:113-115 - Variables width, height, photoData initialized to null without guaranteed initialization before use

findings.name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
findings.description: The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable or unintended results.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:148-149 - width and height may remain null if exception occurs during image dimension extraction, then used in photo.setWidth(width) without null safety checks

findings.name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
findings.description: The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.
findings.evidence: MathUtil.java:15-18 - while (b != 0) loop condition uses modulo operation without input validation

findings.name: Incorrect Calculation
findings.description: The product performs a calculation that generates incorrect or unintended results that are later used in security-critical decisions or resource management.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:94 - Integer division calculation maxFileSizeBytes / 1024 / 1024 may lose precision when displaying MB limit in error messages

findings.name: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
findings.evidence: DetailController.java:72 - Delete operation without security logging for failed operations. No user/principal information logged for security-critical operations.

findings.name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
findings.description: The product allocates memory based on an untrusted, large size value, but it does not ensure that the size is within expected limits, allowing arbitrary amounts of memory to be allocated.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:119 - file.getBytes() called after size check but heap allocation could exceed limits with boundary values

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: application.properties:11-12 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum and spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; docker-compose.yml:30-34 - SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME and SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD hardcoded
